mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in a single month
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.3pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with out-of-sample accuracy below 52% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 1.5% annually indicates robust manufacturing capacity utilization, which drives equity valuations higher only during periods of stable inflation expectations.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth above 1.5% yields negative or flat 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL month-over-month change exceeds 0.4%, invalidating the regime constraint.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index combined with a flat or rising federal funds rate creates a real rate tightening environment that compresses equity multiples and forces negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising CPIAUCSL and non-declining FEDFUNDS produces positive 5-day returns in over 60% of observed cases, negating the tightening penalty effect.

mechanism_narrative: Steady increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and wage growth, which systematically supports corporate revenue forecasts and positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS month-over-month changes show zero predictive power for 60-day return direction, with a cumulative Rank IC below 0.01 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly housing starts figure falling below 900,000 units signals a sharp contraction in construction demand and consumer confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of months where HOUST falls below the 900,000 unit threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between industrial production growth and rising unemployment indicates supply-side inefficiency and economic fragility, triggering rapid risk aversion and negative weekly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns, with a hit rate below 45% and no statistical significance in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.
